join.title: Join your assessment
join.subtitle: Enter the code your employer shared.

assessment.onesitting: Complete every part in one sitting.
assessment.proctored: The aptitude test is timed and proctored via screen share — use a desktop browser.
assessment.bridgeReady: Get ready for the final part — a short voice conversation with Clarion about your experience and approach.

interview.speakNaturally: Speak naturally — you can ask Clarion anything along the way, including exactly how you're being assessed.
interview.howAssessed: How you're assessed — and why it's fair
interview.fairBody: Clarion asks every candidate the same questions and scores against the same rubric. You're judged on what you say — not your school, your background, or who you know.
interview.seeStrong: See exactly what a strong answer looks like
interview.trustFinal: A real person makes the final decision — this is a recommendation, not a verdict.
interview.trustReview: You can request a human review of your interview afterward.
interview.trustScored: Only what you say is scored — never your accent, appearance, mood, or background.
interview.trustAsk: Ask Clarion "what are you assessing me on?" anytime — it'll tell you straight.
interview.recordingNotice: 🔴 This interview is recorded (audio & video) so the employer can review it. Your answers are scored against a fixed rubric — only what you say is assessed. No facial analysis, no demographic inputs.
interview.recordingPrivacy: Your recording is private to {orgName} — never sold, shared, or used to train AI.
interview.preferText: Prefer not to use voice? Take the written interview instead
interview.doneThanks: Thanks, {name}. Your responses have been saved and sent to {org}.
interview.doneSameRubric: You were scored against the same rubric as every other candidate. A member of the hiring team — a real person — makes the final decision; Clarion's assessment is a recommendation, not a verdict.
interview.doneNextBody: {org} reviews your assessment and a real person makes the decision. Bookmark this page — you can return here anytime to see your status.
interview.doneMissed: Feel something was missed or assessed unfairly?
interview.invalidLink: This interview link is invalid or expired
interview.invalidLinkBody: Ask the employer for a new link, or enter your join code.
interview.agentOpening: Hi, thanks for joining! I'm Clarion, an AI interviewer for the {roleTitle} role. I'll ask you {n} questions, taking about {mins} minutes. Every candidate gets the same questions — it's consistent and fair. This is recorded for the hiring team. Ready? Tell me about yourself and your experience.

resume.title: Add your resume
resume.subtitleOrg: has your full background on file.
resume.notice: Your resume is context for the hiring team — it is not scored by the AI. You're assessed on what you demonstrate, not your resume.
resume.skip: Skip — I'd rather be judged on my assessment
resume.errorType: Please upload a PDF, DOC, or DOCX file.
resume.errorSize: That file is over 10 MB — please upload a smaller version.

login.title: Sign in to manage roles
login.subtitle: Employers sign in to author roles and review candidates.

dash.title: Dashboard
dash.subtitle: What needs your attention.
dash.noRoles: No roles yet — build your first assessment.
dash.nothingWaiting: Nothing waiting on you. New completed assessments show up here.

rolesP.title: Roles
rolesP.subtitle: Each role is an assessment. Build it once, send it to every candidate.
rolesP.limitReached: You've reached the 3-role limit for this workspace. Delete a role to create a new one.

candsP.title: Candidates
candsP.subtitle: Drag a candidate to advance or reject them. Each shows Clarion's overall score out of 100.
candsP.noCompleted: No completed candidates yet. Share a role's join code — finished candidates appear here to review.
candsP.resumeClaims: Resume claims probed in the interview
candsP.resumeClaimsNote: Clarion asked the candidate to substantiate these resume claims live. The resume itself is not scored — only what they demonstrated in the interview.

settingsP.title: Settings
settingsP.subtitle: Your workspace and account.
settingsP.interfaceLangDesc: Changes the language of this dashboard and the public landing page. The candidate assessment language is set per-role.
settingsP.workspaceDesc: Your company name appears to candidates on their assessment.

panel.title: Invite candidates
panel.subtitle: Share one code — anyone can join, no need to add them first.
panel.noOne: No one yet — share the code above to start getting candidates.
panel.orInvite: Or invite one specific person by name

wizard.describeSubtitle: Speak it out loud, or type. Mention the role, the key skills, and what great looks like.
wizard.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. I need a customer support rep who stays calm with angry customers, takes ownership of problems, and communicates clearly…
wizard.followupsSubtitle: These sharpen the assessment. Answer briefly.
wizard.reviewSubtitle: Clarion drafted this. Edit anything, then save.
wizard.technicalNote: Voice interviews assess communication and problem-solving approach — consider pairing with a take-home test to evaluate hands-on technical skill.
wizard.micBlocked: Microphone blocked — type your description below instead.
wizard.transcribeFailed: Couldn't transcribe — type your description below instead.
wizard.describeFirst: Describe the role first (speak or type).

form.candidateLanguageDesc: The language candidates see during their interview and assessment. Defaults to your current interface language.
form.scoreAnchors: Score anchors — what each level (1–5) looks like
form.mcqHint: AI drafted these — review every question and mark the correct answer before candidates take the test. Click an option's circle to set it as correct, or run a difficulty check to flag weak/incorrect items.
form.noAptitude: No aptitude questions. Add one, or this part is skipped for candidates.
form.glossaryHint: Terms, tools, and proper nouns the AI interviewer should recognise when a candidate says them aloud — so speech-to-text mishearings (e.g. “cloud code” → “Claude Code”) get corrected. Add any company-specific jargon.
form.resumeHint: Candidates are scored on their assessment, never their resume. This only controls whether you also collect a resume as background for your review.

picker.voiceTitle: Rather just talk? Describe the role by voice
picker.voiceSubtitle: Say what you need in your own words — Clarion builds the whole assessment.
picker.categorySubtitle: Pick an area — you'll choose the exact role next. No long forms.
picker.customRolePlaceholder: Type the role — e.g. Warehouse supervisor, Tutor…
picker.pickClosest: Pick the closest — or add your own.
picker.whichSkills: Which skills matter most for a {role}?
picker.tapSkills: Tap the ones you care about. Add any we missed — Clarion builds the assessment around your picks.
picker.suggestingSkills: Clarion is suggesting skills for this role…

verdict.disclaimer: Clarion assesses. You decide — the verdict is a recommendation, not a decision.
verdict.validityNote: A weighted score out of 100 — not a percentile or class rank. Weights follow each method's predictive validity in the research literature (interview ρ≈.42, skills work-sample ρ≈.40, aptitude screen ρ≈.31, Sackett et al. 2022); these are method-level figures, not a measured validity for this assessment. Treat the band, not the exact number, as the signal — and treat it as a recommendation. You decide.
verdict.appealFlag: ⚑ This candidate has requested a human review of their interview.
verdict.proctorFlagSuffix: Review the screen recording before relying on the aptitude score.
verdict.proctorSharedWindow: shared a window/tab instead of the entire screen
verdict.notCompleted: This candidate hasn't completed their interview yet.
verdict.integrityFlag: ⚑ Possible attempt to game the interview — review the transcript.
verdict.calibrationSubtitle: Rate each criterion to measure how closely Clarion agrees with you — this is your reliability evidence.
verdict.agreementSummary: You & Clarion: exact on {exact}%, within ±1 on {within1}% ({n} criteria · avg diff {mae})
verdict.noRecording: No recording was captured for this interview — the candidate may have declined camera/microphone access or taken the written interview. The full transcript is below.
verdict.proctorRecordingDesc: Proctoring capture from Part 1, so you can confirm the timed test was taken honestly.

landing.heroTag: For small teams who can't afford to hire wrong
landing.heroBody: Clarion gives every candidate the same structured assessment — an aptitude screen, a skills work-sample, and an AI interview — then shows you a ranked verdict with the exact evidence behind every score. You make the final call.
landing.heroFootnote: Set it up in minutes — describe the role by voice or text, edit anything, then send. The same fair assessment for everyone.
landing.heroCaption: Interviewing… “Tell me about a time you calmed an angry customer.”
landing.heroCardJustification: Acknowledged the customer's frustration before problem-solving.
landing.heroCardQuote: “I told her I completely understood why she was upset, and that I'd stay on until it was fixed.”
landing.invitedBody: Enter the code your employer sent you — no account needed.
landing.problemTitle: Small employers hire on the wrong signals.
landing.problemBody: No recruiting team, no time. So screening comes down to resumes, referrals, and gut feel — hiring on social capital, not skill. Good candidates with the wrong network get filtered out, and everyone gets a different interview on a different day from someone in a different mood.
landing.statCost: average cost of one bad hire (CareerBuilder)
landing.statBadHire: of small businesses have made a bad hire
landing.statJobOpenings: job openings per jobseeker in Singapore (MOM, 2025)
landing.pillar1Body: Same assessment, same scoring, every candidate — not how the interviewer felt that day.
landing.pillar2Body: Judged on what they demonstrate, not who they know or where they studied.
landing.pillar3Body: The employer sees the evidence; the candidate knows exactly what they're assessed on.
landing.pillar4Body: No enterprise contract, no recruiter required. Set up a role and start by Friday.
landing.glassBoxTitle: Most AI hiring tools score you in a black box. Clarion shows its work.
landing.glassBoxBody: Every score expands to the evidence behind it — the transcript quote, the written answer, the test result. The AI assesses; the human decides. No mystery verdicts, no unaccountable rejections — for the employer and the candidate.
landing.glassQuote1: “I told her I completely understood why she was upset.”
landing.glassQuote2: “I stayed on the line until the refund actually cleared.”
landing.glassQuote3: “I got a bit flustered but I asked a colleague for help.”
landing.ctaTag: Hear the proof, not the pedigree
landing.ctaBody: Set up a fair, evidence-based assessment in minutes — and decide with proof, not gut feel.

features.title: From a role description to a fair verdict
features.subtitleLine2: Assess everyone the same. Decide with the evidence.
features.card1Prompt: I need a support rep who {calm}, {ownership} of problems, and {clear}.
features.card1Body: Describe the role by voice or text — Clarion drafts the test, work-sample, and questions. Review and edit everything.
features.card2Body: Aptitude screen, skills work-sample, and an adaptive AI interview — the same fair assessment for everyone.
features.card3Quote: “I told her I completely understood why she was frustrated, and I'd stay on the line until it was sorted.”
features.card3Body: Every score links to the exact transcript quote. You make the final call.

samplePage.intro: This is an example of Clarion's glass-box verdict — every score links to the exact words behind it. Expand a criterion to see the evidence highlighted in the transcript.
